asset_name: HUD strip — Winter resources and round status
shared_properties: The strip spans a wide top-center HUD area and keeps all key economy numbers visible during every phase.
Core palette fields remain fixed at primary #111111, background #000000, and accent #FFFFFF.
Large monochrome containers and high-contrast separators support readability without color coding.
differences: The strip contains resource counts, winter index, settlement HP, and population status in one aligned band.
Icon sockets are reserved for crisis glyphs and key resources so urgency can be communicated quickly.
Thin inner dividers keep each information block separated while preserving a single cohesive panel.

reference_images.label: jarl_of_blizzard_moodboard
reference_images.purpose: Grounds the monochrome blizzard mood, Viking settlement silhouettes, and snow density targets.

bevy_constraints.bundle_strategy: Static HUD SpriteBundle anchored to the top center with reserved icon slots and numeric label zones.
bevy_constraints.asset_loading: Load ui/art_000015.png during UI boot and anchor it to the top-center safe area for every gameplay phase.
runtime_notes: Keep the strip in screen space so resource counts never drift with world motion or overlays.
Reserve icon sockets that can display atlas frames from art_000008 without resizing the panel.
Use bitmap-font overlays for numbers and labels rather than painting text into the texture.

context.usage: Persistent top HUD strip for resources, winter number, population, and settlement condition.

shape.silhouette: Wide monochrome HUD band with five resource bays, winter index plate, and settlement-status capsule.

acceptance_criteria: PNG canvas is exactly 1160x96px with clearly separated numeric bays for at least five resources.
Main labels and dividers remain readable over the black panel body at 1x scale.
The panel leaves space for dynamic text and atlas icons without covering the jarl portrait anchor.
